properties.name: resource

options.name: Indices
options.description: Manage your indices and index settings

options.name: Records
options.description: Add, update, and delete records from your indices

options.name: Rules
options.description: Create, update, delete, and search for rules

options.name: Search
options.description: Search one or more indices for matching records or facet values

options.name: Synonyms
options.description: Create, update, delete, and search for synonyms

options.name: Vaults
options.description: Algolia Vault lets you restrict access to your clusters to specific IP addresses and provides disk-level encryption at rest

properties.description: Select the resource to work with

properties.name: operation

options.name: Search an index
options.description: Searches a single index and returns matching search results as hits.

options.name: Search multiple indices
options.description: Sends multiple search requests to one or more indices.

options.name: Search for facet values
options.description: Searches for values of a specified facet attribute.

options.name: Browse for records
options.description: Retrieves records from an index, up to 1,000 per request.

properties.description: Select the operation to work with

properties.name: operation

options.name: Add a new record (with auto-generated object ID)
options.action: Add a new record (with auto-generated object ID)
options.description: Adds a record to an index or replaces it.

options.name: Retrieve a record
options.description: Retrieves one record by its object ID.

options.name: Add or replace a record
options.description: If a record with the specified object ID exists, the existing record is replaced.

options.name: Delete a record
options.description: Deletes a record by its object ID.

options.name: Delete records matching a filter
options.description: This operation doesn't accept empty filters.

options.name: Delete all records from an index
options.action: Delete all records from an index
options.description: Deletes only the records from an index while keeping settings, synonyms, and rules.

options.name: Add or update attributes
options.description: Adds new attributes to a record, or updates existing ones.

options.name: Batch indexing operations on one index
options.action: Batch indexing operations on one index
options.description: Adds, updates, or deletes records in one index with a single API request.

options.name: Batch indexing operations on multiple indices
options.action: Batch indexing operations on multiple indices
options.description: Adds, updates, or deletes records in multiple indices with a single API request.

options.name: Retrieve records
options.description: Retrieves one or more records, potentially from different indices.

properties.description: Select the operation to work with

properties.name: operation

options.name: Delete an index
options.description: Deletes an index and all its settings.

options.name: Retrieve index settings
options.description: Retrieves an object with non-null index settings.

options.name: Check task status
options.description: Checks the status of a given task.

options.name: Copy or move an index
options.description: Copies or moves (renames) an index within the same Algolia application.

options.name: List indices
options.description: Lists all indices in the current Algolia application.

properties.description: Select the operation to work with

properties.name: operation

options.name: Retrieve a synonym
options.description: Retrieves a synonym by its ID.

options.name: Create or replace a synonym
options.description: If a synonym with the specified object ID doesn't exist, Algolia adds a new one.

options.name: Delete a synonym
options.description: Deletes a synonym by its ID.

options.name: Create or replace synonyms
options.description: If a synonym with the `objectID` doesn't exist, Algolia adds a new one.

options.name: Delete all synonyms
options.description: Deletes all synonyms from the index.

options.name: Search for synonyms
options.description: Searches for synonyms in your index.

properties.description: Select the operation to work with

properties.name: operation

options.name: List API keys
options.description: Lists all API keys associated with your Algolia application, including their permissions and restrictions.

options.name: Create an API key

options.name: Retrieve API key permissions
options.description: Gets the permissions and restrictions of an API key.

options.name: Update an API key
options.description: Replaces the permissions of an existing API key.

properties.description: Select the operation to work with

properties.name: operation

options.name: Retrieve a rule
options.description: Retrieves a rule by its ID.

options.name: Create or replace a rule
options.description: If a rule with the specified object ID doesn't exist, it's created.

options.name: Delete a rule
options.description: Deletes a rule by its ID.

options.name: Delete all rules
options.description: Deletes all rules from the index.

options.name: Search for rules
options.description: Searches for rules in your index.

properties.description: Select the operation to work with

properties.name: operation

options.name: Add or delete dictionary entries
options.description: Adds or deletes multiple entries from your plurals, segmentation, or stop word dictionaries.

options.name: Search dictionary entries
options.description: Searches for standard and custom dictionary entries.

options.name: Retrieve dictionary settings
options.description: Retrieves the languages for which standard dictionary entries are turned off.

options.name: Update dictionary settings
options.description: Turns standard stop word dictionary entries on or off for a given language.

options.name: List available languages
options.description: Lists supported languages with their supported dictionary types and number of custom entries.

properties.description: Select the operation to work with

properties.name: operation

options.name: Assign or move a user ID

options.description: Lists the userIDs assigned to a multi-cluster application.

options.name: Assign multiple userIDs
options.description: Assigns multiple user IDs to a cluster.

options.name: Retrieve user ID
options.description: Returns the user ID data stored in the mapping.

options.name: Delete user ID
options.description: Deletes a user ID and its associated data from the clusters.

options.name: List clusters
options.description: Lists the available clusters in a multi-cluster setup.

options.name: Search for user IDs
options.description: Since it can take a few seconds to get the data from the different clusters,

options.name: Get migration and user mapping status
options.action: Get migration and user mapping status
options.description: To determine when the time-consuming process of creating a large batch of users or migrating users from one cluster to another is complete, this operation retrieves the status of the process.

options.name: Get Many
options.description: This will help you to fetch the list of documents in your account

options.name: Get
options.description: This retrieves the details of the document including the status of the document. Use the key “request_status” to check for document completion.

options.name: Get Form Data
options.description: Retrieves the filled field data for a particular document

options.name: Get Folder List
options.description: This will fetch the list of folders in your account

options.name: Get Field Types
options.description: This will help you to retrieve field types

options.name: Get Document Types
options.description: This will help you to find the document types in your account

options.name: Download PDF
options.description: Retrieves the PDF content that can be downloaded

options.name: Download Particular PDF File
options.description: Retrieves the PDF content of a particular file

options.name: Download Completion Certificate
options.description: Retrieves the completion certificate PDF content

options.name: Recall
options.description: Recalling a request cancels the signing process. Once you recall a request, recipients can no longer view or sign it. You can recall a request if you send an incorrect document, or if you send it to an incorrect email address

options.name: Remind Recipient
options.description: A reminder is sent to the recipients who need to sign

options.name: Create a New Folder
options.description: This will help you to create a new folder

options.name: Create New Document Type
options.description: This will help you to create a new document type

options.name: Update Document Type
options.description: This will help you to update an existing document type

options.name: Correct
options.description: This will help you to mark a request, that has been submitted for signatures, as status = correction, so you can edit information of the sent request

options.name: Submit
options.description: This will help you to send a request to your recipients for signing

options.name: Delete
options.description: Deleting a document moves it to trash

options.name: Extend
options.description: This will help you to extend the expiration date of the request signing link

options.name: Update
options.description: This will help you to update information regarding a request

options.name: Get Many
options.description: This will help you to fetch the list of templates in your account

options.name: Get
options.description: Get the details of a particular template using its ID

options.name: Upload
options.description: Upload a document or documents as a template

options.name: Send For Signatures
options.description: Use a template to send a request for signing to the recipients

options.name: Delete
options.description: Deleting a template moves it to trash

options.name: Storage
options.description:
Storage is a separate container for each file. You need to use _Add Storage_ method before adding files to your projects via API. Files that should be uploaded into storage include files for localization, screenshots, Glossaries, and Translation Memories.

_Storage id_ is the identifier of the file uploaded to the Storage.

__Note:__ Files uploaded to the storage are kept during the next 24 hours.

options.name: Languages
options.description:
Crowdin supports more than 300 world languages and custom languages created in the system.

Use API to get the list of all supported languages and retrieve additional details (e.g. text direction, internal code) on specific language.

options.name: Projects
options.description:
Using projects, you can keep your source strings sorted.

Use API to manage projects, change their settings, or remove them if required.

options.name: Branches
options.description:
Source branches are resources for translation. Use API to manage project branches.

__Note:__ Make sure your master branch is the first one you integrate with Crowdin.

options.name: Translations
options.description:
Translators can work with entirely untranslated project or you can pre-translate the files to ease the translations process.

Use API to pre-translate files via Machine Translation (MT) or Translation Memory (TM), upload your existing translations, and download translations correspondingly. Pre-translate and build are [asynchronous operations](#section/Introduction/Asynchronous-Operations) and shall be completed with sequence of API methods.

__Note:__ If there are no new translations or changes in build parameters, Crowdin will return the current build for such requests.

options.name: Source Strings
options.description:
Source strings are the text units for translation. Instead of modifying source files, you can manage source strings one by one.

Use API to add, edit, or delete some specific strings  in the source-based and files-based projects (available only for the following file formats: CSV, RESX, JSON, Android XML, iOS strings, PROPERTIES, XLIFF).

options.name: String Translations
options.description: Use API to add or remove strings translations, approvals, and votes.

options.name: String Comments
options.description: Use API to list, add, edit or remove string comments.

options.name: Screenshots
options.description:
Screenshots provide translators with additional context for the source strings.  Screenshot tags allow specifying  which source strings are displayed on each screenshot.

Use API to manage screenshots and their tags.

options.name: Style Guides
options.description:
Style Guides help to provide additional context for the translators.

Use API to manage style guides.

options.name: Translation Memory
options.description:
Translation Memory (TM) is a vault of translations that were previously made in other projects. Those translations can be reused to speed up the translation process. Every translation made in the project is automatically added to the project Translation Memory.

Use API to create, upload, download, or remove specific TM. Translation Memory export and import are [asynchronous operations](#section/Introduction/Asynchronous-Operations) and shall be completed with sequence of API methods.

options.name: Machine Translation Engines
options.description:
Machine Translation Engines (MTE) are the sources for pre-translations. You can currently connect Google Translate, Microsoft Translator, Crowdin Translate, DeepL Pro and Amazon Translate engines.

Use API to add, update, and delete specific MTE.

options.name: Translation Status
options.description:
Status represents the general localization progress on both translations and proofreading.

Use API to check translation and proofreading progress on different levels: file, language, branch, directory.

options.name: Reports
options.description:
Reports help to estimate costs, calculate translation costs, and identify the top members.

Use API to generate Cost Estimate, Translation Cost, and Top Members reports. You can then export reports in .xlsx or .csv file formats. Report generation is an [asynchronous operation](#section/Introduction/Asynchronous-Operations) and shall be completed with a sequence of API methods.

options.name: Organization Webhooks
options.description:
Webhooks allow you to collect information about events that happen in your Crowdin account. You can select the request type, content type, and add a custom payload, which allows you to create integrations with other systems on your own.

You can configure webhooks for the following events:
 * project is created
 * project is deleted

Use API to create, modify, and delete specific webhooks.

options.name: Dictionaries
options.description:
Dictionaries allow you to create a storage of words that should be skipped by the spell checker.

Use API to get the list of organization dictionaries and to edit a specific dictionary.

options.name: Applications
options.description:
Crowdin Apps are web applications that can be integrated with Crowdin to extend its functionality.

Use the API to manage the necessary app data.

options.name: Glossaries
options.description:
Glossaries help to explain some specific terms or the ones often used in the project so that they can be properly and consistently translated.

Use API to manage glossaries or specific terms. Glossary export and import are [asynchronous operations](#section/Introduction/Asynchronous-Operations) and shall be completed with sequence of API methods.

options.name: Tasks
options.description:
Create and assign tasks to get files translated or proofread by specific people. You can set the due dates, split words between people, and receive notifications about the changes and updates on tasks. Tasks are project-specific, so you’ll have to create them within a project.

Use API to create, modify, and delete specific tasks.

options.name: Users
options.description: Users API gives you the possibility to get profile information about the currently authenticated user.

options.name: Webhooks
options.description:
Webhooks allow you to collect information about events that happen in your Crowdin projects. You can select the request type, content type, and add a custom payload, which allows you to create integrations with other systems on your own.

You can configure webhooks for the following events:
 * all strings in project are translated
 * all strings in project are reviewed
 * all strings in project QA check are finished
 * final translation of string is updated (using Replace in suggestions feature)
 * source string is added
 * source string is updated
 * source string is deleted
 * source string is translated
 * translation for source string is updated (using Replace in suggestions feature)
 * one of translations is deleted
 * translation for string is approved
 * approval for previously added translation is removed

Use API to create, modify, and delete specific webhooks.

options.name: Download Project File Format Settings Custom Segmentation
options.action: Download Project File Format Settings Custom Segmentation

options.name: Reset Project File Format Settings Custom Segmentation
options.action: Reset Project File Format Settings Custom Segmentation

options.name: Merge Branch
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.source.string` (Read and Write).

__NOTE:__ By default, during merge: strings will be added from the source branch, deleted strings will be removed, changed strings will be updated. Conflicted strings will remain unchanged unless `acceptSourceChanges` is enabled.

options.name: Export Project Translation
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.translation` (Read only).

__Note:__ For instant translation delivery to your mobile, web, server, or desktop apps, it is recommended to use [OTA](https://support.crowdin.com/content-delivery/).

options.name: List Translation Approvals
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.translation` (Read only).

__Note:__ Either `translationId` OR `labelIds` OR `excludeLabelIds` with `languageId` OR `stringId` with `languageId` are required

options.name: List Language Translations
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.translation` (Read only).

__Note:__ For instant translation delivery to your mobile, web, server, or desktop apps, it is recommended to use [OTA](https://support.crowdin.com/content-delivery/).

options.name: List Translations
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.translation` (Read only).

__Note:__ For instant translation delivery to your mobile, web, server, or desktop apps, it is recommended to use [OTA](https://support.crowdin.com/content-delivery/).

options.name: List Translation Votes
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.translation` (Read only).

__Note:__ Either `translationId` OR `labelIds` OR `excludeLabelIds` with `languageId` OR `stringId` with `languageId` are required

options.name: List TM Segments
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `tm` (Read only).

Get list of translation unit segments

options.name: Create TM Segment
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `tm` (Read and Write).

Create translation unit with given segments

options.name: Get TM Segment
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `tm` (Read only).

Get segments of translation unit

options.name: Delete TM Segment
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `tm` (Read and Write).

Delete all segments of translation unit

options.name: Edit TM Segment
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `tm` (Read and Write).

Edit segment of translation unit

options.name: Revalidate QA Checks
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.status.qa-check` (Read and Write).

Triggers revalidation of all QA checks for the project

options.name: QA Checks Revalidation Status
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.status.qa-check` (Read and Write).

Returns the status of QA checks revalidation job

options.name: Cancel QA Checks Revalidation
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.status.qa-check` (Read and Write).

Cancels the QA checks revalidation job

options.name: Get Application Data
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `application` (Read only).

Retrieves data from the specified application.

__Note__: Query parameters are application-specific and vary depending on the application being accessed.

options.name: Update or Restore Application Data
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `application` (Read and Write).

Updates or restores data in the specified application.

__Note__: Both the query parameters and request body are application-specific and vary depending on the application being accessed.

options.name: Add Application Data
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `application` (Read and Write).

Adds new data to the specified application.

__Note__: Both the query parameters and request body are application-specific and vary depending on the application being accessed.

options.name: Delete Application Data
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `application` (Read and Write).

Deletes data from the specified application.

__Note__: Query parameters are application-specific and vary depending on the application being accessed.

options.name: Edit Application Data
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `application` (Read and Write).

Edits existing data in the specified application.

__Note__: Both the query parameters and request body are application-specific and vary depending on the application being accessed.

options.name: Update concept
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `glossary` (Read and Write).

__Note:__ There is no separate method for concept creation. Concepts are automatically created when adding a term without specifying a `conceptId`.

options.name: Add Term
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `glossary` (Read and Write).

__Note:__ If `conceptId` is not specified, a new concept will be automatically created for the term.

options.name: List Tasks
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.task` (Read only).

Lists all of the user's project tasks.

nodeDescription.subtitle: ={{$parameter["operation"] + ($parameter["process"] !== undefined ? (": " + $parameter["process"]) : "")}}

options.name: Run Process
options.description: Move your complex business logic into yep code processes and trigger them from your workflows using dynamic input parameters it s the most flexible way to connect with your ap is and services using real code with zero dev ops overhead

options.name: Run Code
options.description: A lightweight, flexible way to execute Node.js or Python code on demand — directly from your workflows or AI agents. The run_code tool runs in secure cloud sandboxes with full support for NPM and PyPI dependencies (https://yepcode.io/docs/dependencies), access to secrets, APIs, and databases. Perfect for quick scripts, dynamic logic, or AI-generated code.

nodeDescription.description: YepCode lets you run full processes or dynamic scripts using Node.js or Python, with support for any NPM or PyPI dependency. All in a secure, sandboxed environment.

attributes.description: YepCode lets you run full processes or dynamic scripts using Node.js or Python, with support for any NPM or PyPI dependency. All in a secure, sandboxed environment.
